evals.name: blog-with-dynamic-route
evals.prompt: I want to start a new Deno Fresh site. Set up the project and add a blog: a /blog page that lists posts read from a local posts.json file (each post has slug, title, date, body), and a /blog/[slug] page that shows a single post's full body. If the slug doesn't exist, show a 404. Give me the actual files.
evals.expected_output: A Fresh 2 project skeleton (main.ts with new App().use(staticFiles()).fsRoutes(), vite.config.ts with @fresh/plugin-vite, utils.ts with createDefine, deno.json) plus routes/blog/index.tsx and routes/blog/[slug].tsx using define.handlers + define.page<typeof handler> + page(), reading params via ctx.params.slug, and throwing HttpError(404) (handled by routes/_error.tsx) for a missing slug.

evals.name: dark-mode-shared-island
evals.prompt: On my Fresh app I need a dark-mode toggle button. The toggle lives in the header but a small 'currently: dark/light' label in the footer needs to reflect the same state and update instantly when I click the toggle. Make it work and explain where the files go.
evals.expected_output: Uses islands (in islands/ or (_islands)/) with Preact signals. A module-level signal() (or a single signal passed as a prop into both the header and footer islands) shares state across the two islands so they stay in sync. Event handler (onClick) lives inside the island, never passed as a prop. No useState; no function props crossing the island boundary.
assertions: No `useState` (signals preferred, and useState can't cross islands)
Toggle and footer label share one signal
Event handler lives inside the island (onClick in island)
No function props passed across the island boundary

evals.name: contact-form-and-protected-admin
evals.prompt: Add two things to my Deno Fresh app: (1) a /contact page with a form (name + email + message) that validates the email server-side and, on success, redirects to /thanks; (2) a /admin section that's only accessible if there's a logged-in session, otherwise it should bounce to /login. Show me the route and middleware files.
evals.expected_output: A POST handler reading await ctx.req.formData(), validating server-side, returning a 303 redirect to /thanks on success (Post/Redirect/Get) and re-rendering with an error otherwise. A routes/admin/_middleware.ts (or equivalent define.middleware) that checks session (cookie/ctx.state) and returns ctx.redirect('/login') when absent, else await ctx.next(). Single (ctx) handler/middleware signature throughout; State typed via define.
assertions: Server-side email validation in the contact handler
